_comment: Curated runtime-version spoofs. When a transformed mod calls FabricLoader.getModContainer(id) for one of these mod IDs, VersionSpoofer returns a wrapped container whose reported version is the string below. Only the version-reporting chain is lied about - every other container field passes through real data. A missing mod (no installed container) passes through as Optional.empty, so the spoofer never fabricates versions for absent mods.
_design_rule: Only spoof when the real installed version is GENUINELY incompatible with a common dependent check. If a user has the current major release of a library installed (e.g. Cloth Config 15.x on MC 1.21+), modern dependents already accept it - spoofing downgrades that into artificial incompatibility. The right default is: DO NOT SPOOF unless we know the user's version won't naturally pass.

_removed_in_v2.cloth-config: Current users run Cloth 15.x which REI 16 accepts natively. Spoofing to 13.x broke REI 16 for people with newer Cloth installed.
_removed_in_v2.cloth-config2: Same rationale - 13.x spoof broke modern setups.
_removed_in_v2.architectury: REI 16 bundles compatible Architectury as a nested JAR; spoofing to 13.x caused REI to reject its own bundle.
_removed_in_v2.roughlyenoughitems: Current versions satisfy most dependent checks naturally.
_removed_in_v2.trinkets: Users typically install the current version; real version already works.

_kept_in_v2_why: These spoofs remain because the real installed version on a 26.1.2 setup is genuinely newer than what most old mods expect. Spoofing DOWN is the correct move here - dependent mods that declare '<X.Y.Z' upper bounds on the core API families will see a plausibly-old version and accept it. A user who installs a current library is actively asking for that library; we're only intervening for mods that lock to historical ranges of CORE platform families.
